skill.name: brewpage-publish
skill.description: Publish text, Markdown, JSON, files, or built static sites to brewpage.app and return a shareable public URL.
skill.summary: Publish selected content to brewpage.app with optional namespace, password, TTL, and owner-token history.
skill.author: kochetkov-ma

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, network, filesystem, and sensitive-file patterns. Review found these patterns are largely intentional publishing instructions, with no evidence of prompt injection or hidden malicious behavior, but the skill sends user-selected content to an external public hosting service and stores owner tokens locally.

medium_findings.title: External Public Publishing via Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally uses Bash and curl to send text, JSON, files, ZIP archives, and site archives to brewpage.app. This is core functionality, but it can expose private user content if the user selects the wrong file or directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are explicit curl POST commands to brewpage.app and are part of the documented publish workflow. The risk is data exposure through intended behavior, not covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Owner Tokens Stored in Local Hidden History File
medium_findings.description: The workflow writes owner tokens to .claude/brewpage-history.md for later deletion. The documentation warns that this file is private, but storing bearer-style delete tokens in a workspace file creates leakage risk through commits, logs, or sharing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path and token write behavior are directly documented. The skill includes warnings, which reduce but do not remove the risk.

medium_findings.title: Directory Archiving Can Publish Local Files
medium_findings.description: The site flow zips a user-selected directory and uploads it. It excludes common secret, VCS, dependency, editor, sourcemap, and log paths, but any sensitive file outside those patterns could still be published.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The zip command and upload are explicit, and the exclusions confirm awareness of secret-leak risk. Remaining exposure depends on user-selected directory contents.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Expected for Hosting Integration
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged hardcoded URLs in documentation and curl examples. These point to the declared brewpage.app service and the source repository, so they are expected for this publishing skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs match the documented hosting service and repository identity. No unrelated or hidden endpoint was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Windows SAM Alerts Are Scanner False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain Markdown text, security notes, or secret-exclusion documentation rather than cryptographic code or Windows credential database access. No evidence found of weak hash usage or SAM database access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context around the flagged lines shows prose and placeholders, not crypto APIs or credential database paths. The scanner likely matched short substrings inside normal documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Execution Required for Publishing
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to execute Bash blocks that generate passwords, call curl, parse responses with jq, create ZIP archives, and append history entries.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and operational. They are intended behavior but require user trust in local shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network Uploads
dangerous_patterns.description: The curl commands upload user-provided content and archives to brewpage.app endpoints. Users should understand that publication sends data outside the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and transmitted data paths are visible in the curl examples. This is a clear network upload pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive File Exclusion Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The archive command excludes .git, .env files, node_modules, editor directories, sourcemaps, and logs. This is a protective pattern, but it confirms the workflow handles directories that may contain sensitive local data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exclusion list is directly present and reduces risk. Confidence is lower because the pattern is defensive rather than inherently malicious.

content.value_statement: Sharing temporary pages and files often requires manual hosting setup. This skill publishes selected content to brewpage.app and returns a public URL with optional password protection.
actual_capabilities: Publishes text and Markdown through the brewpage.app HTML endpoint.
Publishes JSON objects and arrays through the brewpage.app JSON endpoint.
Uploads individual local files with multipart form requests.
Packages built static directories into ZIP archives before site upload.
Prompts for namespace, password choice, TTL, and optional site entry file.
Stores owner tokens in a local history file for later deletion.
limitations: Published content is sent to an external service and may become public.
Directory publishing requires built static output with at least one HTML file.
The skill depends on local shell tools such as curl, jq, zip, file, and tr.
Owner tokens are stored locally and must be protected from version control.

use_cases.title: Share a Draft Page
use_cases.description: Publish a short Markdown draft or HTML note and send a temporary URL for review.

use_cases.title: Publish a Static Demo
use_cases.description: Upload a built static directory from dist, build, out, _site, or public for quick stakeholder testing.

use_cases.title: Distribute a Temporary File
use_cases.description: Upload a screenshot, report, or archive with a short TTL and optional password protection.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Simple Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish this note to brewpage with the default TTL: Meeting notes for the release review.

prompt_templates.title: Publish a Markdown File
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload report.md to brewpage and use a readable namespace if one is suggested.

prompt_templates.title: Publish a Site Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy ./dist as a brewpage site with entry index.html and TTL 7 days.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Protected Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish ./build to brewpage with a custom namespace, password protection, and TTL 1 day.

output_examples.input: Publish a Markdown status update to brewpage.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for namespace and password choices, publishes the content, and returns a public brewpage URL.

output_examples.input: Deploy ./dist as a site with TTL 7 days.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks for built HTML output, uploads the archive, returns the site URL, and records the owner token locally.
The assistant sends the selected file to brewpage.app and returns a password-protected share link.

best_practices: Review files before publishing because uploaded content may be public.
Publish built output directories instead of source project directories.
Keep .claude/brewpage-history.md private and out of version control.
anti_patterns: Do not publish secrets, credentials, environment files, or private customer data.
Do not upload a full repository when only build output should be shared.
Do not paste owner tokens into chat, tickets, logs, or shared documents.

faq.question: What does this skill publish?
faq.answer: It publishes text, Markdown, JSON, single files, ZIP files, and built static site directories to brewpage.app.

faq.question: Does it require an account?
faq.answer: The skill documentation says brewpage.app publishing does not require sign-up.

faq.question: Can I protect a page with a password?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill asks whether to use no password, a generated password, or a custom password.

faq.question: Where is the owner token stored?
faq.answer: The skill stores owner tokens in .claude/brewpage-history.md so published items can be deleted later.

faq.question: Can it publish source projects directly?
faq.answer: It is designed to reject or warn on unbuilt source directories and prefers built static output.

faq.question: What tools must be available locally?
faq.answer: The workflow uses Bash and common tools such as curl, jq, zip, file, tr, and shell tests.
